source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure API Keys Are Restricted To Use by Only Specified Hosts and Apps

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure API Keys Are Restricted To Use by Only Specified Hosts and Apps
input_row.description: API Keys should only be used for services in cases where other authentication methods are unavailable. In this case, unrestricted keys are insecure because they can be viewed publicly, such as from within a browser, or they can be accessed on a device where the key resides. It is recommended to restrict API key usage to trusted hosts, HTTP referrers and apps. It is recommended to use the more secure standard authentication flow instead.
input_row.rationale: Security risks involved in using API-Keys appear below: • API keys are simple encrypted strings • API keys do not identify the user or the application making the API request • API keys are typically accessible to clients, making it easy to discover and steal an API key In light of these potential risks, Google recommends using the standard authentication flow instead of API keys. However, there are limited cases where API keys are more appropriate. For example, if there is a mobile application that needs to use the Google Cloud Translation API, but doesn't otherwise need a backend server, API keys are the simplest way to authenticate to that API. In order to reduce attack vectors, API-Keys can be restricted only to trusted hosts, HTTP referrers and applications. Impact: Setting Application Restrictions may break existing application functioning, if not done carefully.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to APIs & Services\Credentials using https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials 2. In the section API Keys, Click the API Key Name. The API Key properties display on a new page. 3. For every API Key, ensure the section Key restrictions parameter Application restrictions is not set to None. Or, 1. Ensure Application restrictions is set to HTTP referrers and the referrer is not set to wild-cards (* or *.[TLD] or *.[TLD]/*) allowing access to any/wide HTTP referrer(s) Or, 1. Ensure Application restrictions is set to IP addresses and referrer is not set to any host (0.0.0.0 or 0.0.0.0/0 or ::0) From Google Cloud Command Line 1. Run the following from within the project you wish to audit gcloud services api-keys list --filter="-restrictions:*" -- format="table[box](displayName:label='Key With No Restrictions')
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Leaving Keys in Place 1. Go to APIs & Services\Credentials using https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials 2. In the section API Keys, Click the API Key Name. The API Key properties display on a new page. 3. In the Key restrictions section, set the application restrictions to any of HTTP referrers, IP addresses, Android apps, iOS apps. 4. Click Save. 5. Repeat steps 2,3,4 for every unrestricted API key. Note: Do not set HTTP referrers to wild-cards (* or *.[TLD] or .[TLD]/ ) allowing access to any/wide HTTP referrer(s) Do not set IP addresses and referrer to any host (0.0.0.0 or 0.0.0.0/0 or ::0) Removing Keys Another option is to remove the keys entirely. 1. Go to APIs & Services\Credentials using https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials 2. In the section API Keys, select the checkbox next to each key you wish to remove 3. Select Delete and confirm. Default Value: By default, Application Restrictions are set to None. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys 2. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/services/api-keys/list 3. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/services/api-keys/update
input_row.impact: Setting Application Restrictions may break existing application functioning, if not done carefully.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control checks if API keys in GCP are restricted to specific hosts, HTTP referrers, or applications. This is a property that can be queried and verified using the GCP API services, particularly with the `gcloud` command-line tool. The audit steps provided include specific `gcloud` commands to check for API keys without restrictions, indicating that this control can be automated.

- ✓ Can GCP APIs query this property? → Yes, through GCP APIs and `gcloud` commands.
- ✓ Is this a standard GCP service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Yes, it involves access control of API keys.
- ✓ Does Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK have methods to check this? → Yes, by using `gcloud services api-keys list`.
- ✓ Does GCP CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Yes, through the `gcloud` command mentioned in the audit instructions.
- ✓ Does audit mention Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands? → Yes, it explicitly mentions `gcloud services api-keys list` for checking API key restrictions.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_services_api_keys_restrictions_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP API Call or Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK Methods to Use:** The `gcloud` command mentioned in the audit instructions: `gcloud services api-keys list --filter="-restrictions:*" --format="table[box](displayName:label='Key With No Restrictions')"` is used to list all API keys that do not have any restrictions.
  
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:** Enumerate all API keys within a GCP project.
  
- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:** Verify that each API key has application restrictions set to either HTTP referrers, IP addresses, Android apps, or iOS apps, and not set to wild-cards or any host which would allow unrestricted access.
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - **Pass:** API keys are restricted to specific HTTP referrers, IP addresses, or mobile apps and do not use wildcards or unrestricted settings.
  - **Fail:** API keys have no application restrictions or misuse wildcards, leaving them unrestricted. 

By using the provided `gcloud` commands and further verification of key restrictions through the Google Cloud SDK or API, this control can be fully automated and does not require manual inspection.
